creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Eugene Grosbein from comment #5)
> I do not understand why correct specification of needed version for dependency
> could be ever removed?
Because it comes with a price.  Once it's there, it should (ideally) be checked and updated upon every port update so it stays accurate.  This creates additional, needless maintenance burden for such a marginal gain that no one would eagerly be doing it.  Implicit (and even explicit) version requirements for dependencies are all over the place, but we don't record them because doing so does not scale.  Instead, we expect that people either use binary packages which are always coherent, or, if they don't want to, like you or me, at least update their installed ports once a year or so.
